title: Owool Interceptor
content:
{{Top|ga|leg}} {{StarshipClass |image=[[File:OwoolInterceptor-EGTW.jpg]] |name=Owool Interceptor |designer= |manufacturer=[[KashyCorp]]''[[Legacy of the Force: Inferno]]'' |line= |model= |type=Heavy [[Starfighter/Legends|starfighter]] |cost= |modifier= |sysmods= |length= |width= |height= |mass= |max accel= |mglt= |max speed= |maneuverability= |engine=2 |hyperdrive=[[Hyperdrive/Legends|Equipped]]In ''[[Legacy of the Force: Inferno]]'', the [[Jedi Coalition]]'s [[Starfighter/Legends|starfighter]] task force at the [[Second Battle of Roche]] does not launch from a [[Capital ship/Legends|capital ship]], thus requiring [[Hyperdrive/Legends|hyperdrives]] to reach the [[Roche system]]. Owool Interceptors were to fly with the task force before being ordered to stand down, indicating that they, too, have hyperdrives. |hdrange= |hdsystem= |poweroutput= |power= |shield gen= |hull= |sensor= |target= |navigation= |avionics= |maincomp= |countermeasures= |armament=*4 [[Laser cannon/Legends|laser cannons]]''[[The Essential Guide to Warfare]]'' *2 [[Ion cannon/Legends|ion cannons]] |complement= |bays= |escapepods= |crew=1 [[Pilot/Legends|pilot]] |skeleton= |passengers= |capacity= |cargohandling=[[Jedi shadow bomb]] |consumables= |lifesupport= |communications= |othersystems= |availability= |role= |firstuse=c. [[40 ABY/Legends|40 ABY]]''[[The Essential Reader's Companion]]'' |retired= |affiliation=*[[Wookiee/Legends|Wookiees]] *[[Jedi Coalition]]''[[Legacy of the Force: Invincible]]'' }} {{Dialogue|Twizzl|What happened to the Owools?|Bimm ensign|I think they got in the way of a turbolaser strike, sir. They were there before the last strike, then they were just… gone.|Caedus|That's not what happened. They're still there. I sense them coming.|Twizzl|Incoming starfighters! Arm the cluster bombs and activate the auto cannons, all ship-defense systems fire at will!|Bimm ensign|I don't understand. Do Owools have stealth…|Caedus|No. They're riding the stripe.|attr=Darth Caedus, [[Commander/Legends|Commander]] [[Twizzl]], and a [[Unidentified Bimm ensign|Bimm ensign]], aboard the ''Anakin Solo''|src=}} The '''Owool Interceptor''' was a heavy, single-[[Pilot/Legends|pilot]] [[Starfighter/Legends|starfighter]] that was designed by the [[Wookiee/Legends|Wookiees]] of the [[Shipyard/Legends|shipyard]] [[KashyCorp]] after the [[Battle of Tenupe|end]] of the [[Swarm War]] in [[36 ABY]]. Operational during the [[Second Galactic Civil War]], the [[Interceptor/Legends|interceptors]] were intended for use by the [[Galactic Federation of Free Alliances|Galactic Alliance]], but when the [[New Jedi Order]] withdrew from the Galactic Alliance, the Wookiees withdrew their support as well, leaving the Owool Interceptors to be taken up by Wookiee pilots instead. Some Wookiees flew the heavy starfighters for the [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] when the Galactic Alliance [[Battle of Kashyyyk (Second Galactic Civil War)|set]] portions of the Wookiee [[Homeworld/Legends|homeworld]] of [[Kashyyyk/Legends|Kashyyyk]] [[Fire/Legends|ablaze]], and later at the culminating [[Battle of Uroro Station|battle]] of the war near the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Shedu Maad]]. ==Characteristics== Owool Interceptors were twin-[[Engine/Legends|engine]] heavy [[Starfighter/Legends|starfighters]] that featured an armament of four [[Laser cannon/Legends|laser cannons]] and two [[Ion cannon/Legends|ion cannons]], and had the ability to launch [[Jedi shadow bomb]]s if required. Operated by a single [[Pilot/Legends|pilot]], the Owool had enough [[Cockpit/Legends|cockpit]] room for a 2.26-[[Meter/Legends|meter]]-tall[[The Official Star Wars Fact File 74|''The Official Star Wars Fact File'' 74]] [[Wookiee/Legends|Wookiee]], with the cockpit situated in the tapered fuselage of the [[Hyperdrive/Legends|hyperdrive]]-equipped [[Color/Legends|brown]] starfighter. With a pair of engine mounts—each of which also housed the [[Laser/Legends|laser]] [[Cannon/Legends|cannons]]—that were connected via a spar to either side of the fuselage and rose slightly above the cockpit, the starfighter looked like a fork-prowed wedge when viewed from the front. Military observers described the fighters as tough, fast, and ferocious like the Wookiees who built them.{{CSWECite|II|433|Owool Interceptor}} ==Role== The Owool Interceptor was used both to attack enemy [[Capital ship/Legends|capital ships]] and in a [[Starfighter combat/Legends|starfighter-versus-starfighter]] capacity, and it was capable of flying fighter escort for other [[Starship/Legends|starships]]. ==History== ===Protecting a homeworld=== {{Quote|Those Owools are threatening to open fire if we don't stop and explain ourselves.''"
"''You do have to respect their courage.|Commander Twizzl and Darth Caedus, aboard the ''Anakin Solo''|}} [[File:Battle_of_Kashyyyk_by_Darren_Tan.jpg|thumb|right|200px|The Battle of Kashyyyk during the Second Galactic Civil War saw Owool Interceptors launched against the Galactic Alliance Fifth Fleet.]] Owool Interceptors were designed and developed in the [[Standard year/Legends|years]] following the events of the [[Swarm War]]—which [[Battle of Tenupe|ended]] in [[36 ABY]]—by the Wookiees of the new [[Shipyard/Legends|shipyard]] [[KashyCorp]]. The first Owools were finished circa [[40 ABY/Legends|40 ABY]], when the [[Galactic Federation of Free Alliances|Galactic Alliance]] was fighting the [[Confederation]] in the [[Second Galactic Civil War]]. Although KashyCorp was supposed to deliver a shipment of the heavy starfighters to the Galactic Alliance, the Wookiee [[Rock Council]] stopped supporting the Alliance when the [[New Jedi Order]] withdrew its support, preventing the Owools from being delivered. In 40 [[Galactic Standard Calendar|ABY]], the Galactic Alliance's [[Chief of State (Galactic Alliance)|Chief of State]], [[Dark Lord of the Sith/Legends|Sith Lord]] [[Darth Caedus]], took the [[Fifth Fleet (Galactic Alliance)|Galactic Alliance Fifth Fleet]] to the Wookiee [[Homeworld/Legends|homeworld]] of [[Kashyyyk/Legends|Kashyyyk]], which was a member of the Alliance. There, Caedus demanded that the Wookiees turn over his [[Parent/Legends|parents]], [[Han Solo/Legends|Han Solo]] and [[Leia Organa Solo/Legends|Leia Organa]], as they were aligned with the [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] who had abandoned the Galactic Alliance, to give Caedus a pretense to attack Kashyyyk when they refused; Caedus aimed to force the [[Confederation Fleet]] to aid the Wookiees instead of continuing its drive for the Galactic Alliance's [[Capital/Legends|capital]] [[Planet/Legends|planet]] of [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]]. With the Fifth Fleet over Kashyyyk, a [[Squadron/Legends|squadron]] of Wookiee-flown Owool Interceptors, led by the Wookiee [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knight]] [[Lowbacca]], was dispatched to meet the capital ships. When the Wookiees refused to hand over Solo and Organa, Caedus' [[Flagship/Legends|flagship]], the [[Imperial II-class Star Destroyer/Legends|''Imperial''-class Star Destroyer]] [[Anakin Solo (starship)|''Anakin Solo'']], [[Orbital bombardment/Legends|opened fire]] upon the [[Tree/Legends|forests]] of Kashyyyk, setting [[Wroshyr tree/Legends|Wroshyr trees]] [[Fire/Legends|ablaze]]. In response to the attack, the Owool squadron, whose fighters amounted to half the number of capital ships in the Fifth Fleet, [[Battle of Kashyyyk (Second Galactic Civil War)|attacked]] the ''Anakin Solo''. Led by Lowbacca, the Owools rode the ''Anakin Solo''{{'s}} stripe, flying up the larger vessel's firing lane toward the [[Bridge (starship)/Legends|bridge]] and using the flash-static of the [[Imperial-class Star Destroyer/Legends|Star Destroyer's]] [[Turbolaser/Legends|turbolasers]] to shield them from its [[Sensor/Legends|sensors]]. Although some of the Owools fell prey to the capital ship's anti-starfighter weapons, Lowbacca scored a direct hit on the bridge with a shadow bomb, causing significant damage. In the [[Space warfare/Legends|fighting]] that followed, the Jedi attacked Caedus' forces for the first [[Time/Legends|time]] in defense of Kashyyyk, and the [[Fleet/Legends|fleet]] elements of the Jedi and their allies, as well as those of the Confederation, drove the Fifth Fleet away. ===End of a war=== {{Quote|Master Horn, please have the Owools stand down.''"
"''The Owools? Just the Owools?''"
"''That's correct. All other elements of the mission will launch as planned.|Jedi Grand Master Luke Skywalker and [[Jedi Master/Legends|Master]] [[Corran Horn/Legends|Corran Horn]], at the secret Jedi base on Shedu Maad|}} In [[41 ABY]], Wookiee-flown Owools were present in the [[Hapes Cluster/Legends|Hapes Cluster]] within the [[Hangar/Legends|hangar]] of a [[Hapan Battle Dragon]] [[Unidentified Hapan Battle Dragon|warship]] that was serving as a launch point for an upcoming [[Jedi Coalition]] [[Second Battle of Roche|operation]] in the [[Roche system]] designed to have Caedus' [[Gender/Legends|sister]], Jedi Knight [[Jaina Solo Fel|Jaina Solo]], [[Assassin/Legends|assassinate]] the [[Sith Lord/Legends|Sith Lord]]. The Owool Interceptors were meant to fly fighter escort for [[Skipray 24r Blastboat]]s in the mission, but Jedi [[Grand Master/Legends|Grand Master]] [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]] deemed them unnecessary and told them to stand down, as he predicted a [[Mandalorian/Legends|fighter escort]] would be present in the Roche [[Star system/Legends|system]] for the [[Blastboat/Legends|blastboats]]. After Caedus survived the attempt on his life and the Galactic Alliance emerged victorious in the Roche system, Caedus [[Blood trail|tracked]] the Jedi to their [[Jedi Academy (Shedu Maad)|secret base]] on the planet [[Shedu Maad]] in the Hapes Cluster's [[Transitory Mists]], bringing with him the ''Anakin Solo'' and the [[Super-class Star Destroyer|''Super''-class Star Destroyer]] ''[[Megador]]'', as well as an [[Imperial Remnant]] fleet. With [[Uroro Station]]—a [[Space station/Legends|space station]] located near Shedu Maad—acting as a decoy for the Jedi base, a squadron of Owool Interceptors launched from the station as part of the starfighter group that [[Battle of Uroro Station|struck]] at the inbound ''Anakin Solo'' and ''Megador''. However, the Owools and the other Jedi Coalition fighters met resistance from the two capital ships' starfighter complements, resulting in a concentrated dogfight forming near the ''Anakin Solo'' and the ''Megador''. In the end, Caedus was [[Death/Legends|slain]] by his sister, while the Jedi Coalition forces prevailed over Caedus' fleet elements, bringing an end to the Second Galactic Civil War. ==Behind the scenes== [[File:Owoolinterceptor-Sketch.jpg|thumb|right|200px|Concept sketches for the Owool Interceptor by Darren Tan for ''The Essential Guide to Warfare''.]] Owool Interceptors first appeared in [[Legacy of the Force: Inferno|''Inferno'']], the sixth novel of the ''[[Star Wars: Legacy of the Force|Legacy of the Force]]'' series, written by [[Troy Denning]] and released in [[2007]]. The heavy starfighters were first illustrated by [[Darren Tan]] in [[2012]]'s ''[[The Essential Guide to Warfare]]''. ==Appearances== *''[[Legacy of the Force: Inferno]]'' {{1st}} *[[Legacy of the Force: Inferno (abridged audiobook)|''Legacy of the Force: Inferno'' abridged audiobook]] *''[[Legacy of the Force: Invincible]]'' *[[Legacy of the Force: Invincible (abridged audiobook)|''Legacy of the Force: Invincible'' abridged audiobook]] ==Sources== *''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'' *''[[The Essential Guide to Warfare]]'' {{1stp}} ==Notes and references== {{Reflist}} ==External links== *{{Facebook|username=starwarsbooks|url=starwarsbooks/photos/a.10150411766083713/10150423899523713/?type=3&theater|name=[[Del Rey|Star Wars Books]]|date=November 21, 2011|archivefile=File:Owoolinterceptor-Sketch.jpg}} {{Interlang |de=Owool-Abfangjäger |es=Interceptor Owool }} [[Category:KashyCorp products]] [[Category:Starfighter classes]] [[Category:Starship classes of the Jedi Order]] [[Category:Wookiee vehicle classes]]
